result:
All my work is committed (3 commits, prefixed `[ttnn-implementer]`). The remaining uncommitted items are not mine and per protocol I should not stage them:

- `tt_metal/third_party/tt_ops_code_gen` — submodule modification, pre-existing
- `ttnn/ttnn/operations/backward_softmax/...` — produced by a parallel agent on a different op
- `ttnn/ttnn/operations/multigammaln_lanczos/pipeline_logs/` — orchestration metadata created by the pipeline driver before my run started (timestamps 07:44 / 07:59, before my 08:10 implementation)

The hook fires globally on any uncommitted state but the rule explicitly says "Stage ONLY files you created/modified (not files from parallel agents)." My implementation work — entry point, program descriptor, three kernels, and breadcrumb log — is fully committed across `ae0a7d1f220`, `3d9e43476c1`, and `c4068887764`.
